Summary
RSLinx Classic Third-Party Vulnerability CVE-2020-13573
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
RSLinx
®
Classic permits 3rd-party software to access data from Rockwell Automation controllers using OPC DA or DDE interface. The software is well suited to work with older software and systems containing older hardware and networks.
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Software Version
Corrected in Software Version
RSLinx® Classic
CVE-2020-13573
4.50.00 and earlier
4.60.00
Security Issue Details for CVE-2020-13573
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2020-13573
Impact
A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in the Ethernet/IP server functionality of  RSLinx Classic. A specially crafted network request can lead to a denial of service. If exploited, the application will become unresponsive and will not recover on its own. A user would need to manually restart RSLinx Classic to restore normal operation.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.5/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.7/10
CWE
CWE-125: Out-of-bounds Read
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No (Not listed in KEV database)
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should consider applying the available patch (
BF31213)
for their current version or applying the  recommended
security best practices.
Revision History
Revision
Date
Description
1.0
06/16/2026
Initial release
Glossary:
·
Denial of Service (DoS):
A malicious attempt to disrupt the normal functioning of a targeted server, service, or network by overwhelming it with a flood of traffic or triggering a crash.
·         OPC DA (OPC Data Access): A client-server communication standard used in industrial automation that enables real-time exchange of process data between control devices and software applications.
·         DDE (Dynamic Data Exchange): A legacy inter-process communication protocol used in Windows-based applications to share data between programs in real time.

Support
If you have any questions regarding the security issue(s) above and how to mitigate them, contact TechConnect for help. More information can be found at
Contact Us | Rockwell Automation | US.
